title: QA: permissions enabled=false by default disables all checks (config/permissions.rs)
description:
## Severity
Critical
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/config/permissions.rs:7-17
## Violation
The default `PermissionsConfig` ships with the entire permission system
disabled:
```rust
pub struct PermissionsConfig {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub enabled: bool,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub default_allow: Vec<String>,
}
```
`bool::default()` is `false`, so any deployment that omits the field — or
ships a fresh `.openclaudia/config.yaml` — runs with
`PermissionManager::check` short-circuiting to `CheckResult::Allowed` at
`permissions.rs:132-134`. The existing pin test
`b7_disabled_allows_all_including_destructive` documents this gap
(`rm -rf /` allowed). Violates secure-by-default (OWASP A05).
## Mandated refactor
Flip the default to `enabled: true`. If a permissive mode is needed for
the proxy-only use case, gate it behind an explicit
`PermissionsConfig::permissive()` constructor that advertises the risk in
its name. Document the breaking change in CHANGELOG and add a startup
log: `warn!("permission system disabled — all tool calls allowed")` when
`enabled == false` so operators always see it.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
